heroSubtitle: Most Indian businesses lose 60–80% of qualified leads to competitors with sharper digital infrastructure. Not better products. Better websites.

webFoundation.title: Web Foundation
webFoundation.subtitle: The performance layer
webFoundation.description: Conversion-architected pages built for India’s mobile-first traffic — fast loads, strong rankings, one clear action.

automation.title: Automation Layer
automation.subtitle: The operations layer
automation.description: Lead capture, CRM routing, WhatsApp follow-ups. Your site works while your team sleeps.

execution.title: Execution Engine
execution.subtitle: The compound growth layer
execution.description: Weekly iteration, A/B tests, SEO compounding — a system that improves every month.

invisibilityTax.heading: Profitable businesses are bleeding revenue online, every day.
invisibilityTax.scenarioIntro: Here is a scenario most founders recognize but rarely quantify.
invisibilityTax.scenarioP1: A serious buyer searches for your service. They find a competitor first, not because the competitor is better, but because their website loads in 1.2 seconds instead of 6.4. Their headline answers the buyer's question. Their CTA is visible above the fold. Your site is not.
invisibilityTax.scenarioP2: You never knew the lead existed. The competitor closed the deal.
invisibilityTax.scenarioP3: This is not a marketing problem. It is an infrastructure problem.
invisibilityTax.stat1Caption: of users judge a company's credibility based on their website design.
invisibilityTax.stat2Caption: of online consumers are less likely to return to a site after a bad experience.
invisibilityTax.stat3Caption: delay in page load time reduces conversions by 7% per second of delay.
invisibilityTax.stat4Caption: more qualified leads generated by businesses with SEO-optimized, high-performance websites vs. those without.
invisibilityTax.closingQuote: Not having a great website is not a cost you can see on a spreadsheet. It is the revenue that never appeared.
invisibilityTax.cta: Build the infrastructure that captures it

paradox.bodyIntro: Every thriving offline business has a ceiling.
paradox.bodyP1: Word of mouth scales until it does not. Referrals dry up. A competitor opens with a $40/month website and begins capturing every search you never knew existed.
paradox.bodyP2: The Michelin-starred restaurant that has no reservations page loses bookings to the decent bistro with OpenTable integration. The contractor who does not rank locally loses to a newer firm with a $3,000 website and 12 Google reviews.
paradox.bodyP4: The question is never "Do I need a website?" The question is: "How much have I already lost by waiting?"

socialProof.heading: Executed for founders, operators, and serious brands.

manifesto.headingLine2: Most businesses just never engineered theirs.

phase1.name: Audit & Deconstruction
phase1.line: Technical audit, SEO structure, conversion gaps, competitor matrix. Benchmarks before pixels.

phase2.name: Architecture & Strategy
phase2.line: IA, conversion-first hierarchy, URL + CMS architecture, keyword topology so every page maps to a metric.

phase3.name: Design System & UI
phase3.line: Token-level system in Figma, components, prototypes with rules that scale beyond launch.

phase4.name: Build & Optimization
phase4.line: Next.js engineering, CMS, analytics, QA shipped toward sub-1.5s LCP targets.

phase5.name: Launch & Compound Growth
phase5.line: Monitoring, weekly iteration, SEO + CRO compounding. Launch is the baseline, not the finish.

items.question: Why Torpedo Web over a freelancer or agency?
items.answer: Most freelancers optimize for deliverables. Traditional agencies optimize for hours. Torpedo Web optimizes for infrastructure: performance budgets, conversion architecture, technical SEO as defaults, analytics baselines, and iteration rhythms you can run as a system, not a one-off project.

items.question: Can you improve an existing website without rebuilding it?
items.answer: Yes. We start with technical and conversion audits, fix structural bottlenecks, tighten information architecture, and instrument analytics. We recommend a full rebuild only when your stack or CMS prevents the latency, SEO, or conversion targets you need.

items.question: What should I prepare before a discovery call?
items.answer: Bring your primary URL, current traffic channels, conversion goal (lead, booking, sale), tools (CRM, forms, ads), and any metrics you trust, even rough ones. We translate that into a prioritized system map, not a generic slide deck.

items.question: Do you work with Indian businesses or only international?
items.answer: Both. We operate US and India with local phone lines and delivery workflows suited to each market. Engineering standards and performance targets are the same; tactics adapt to your geography and search intent.

items.question: What makes your websites actually perform in search?
items.answer: Technical SEO is treated as infrastructure: URL topology, schema, crawl clarity, internal linking, Core Web Vitals as a ranking lever, and content mapped to intent, not a checklist pasted at the end.

items.question: I have a successful business already. Why do I need a better website now?
items.answer: Word of mouth scales until it stops. Competitors capture search demand you never see. The question is not whether you need a digital front door. It is how much revenue has already rerouted to faster, clearer, better-instrumented operators while you waited.
